subject: Human Resources
title: Drug Testing and Substance Policies
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Drug Testing and Substance Policies.

hook.text: A truck driver tests positive for marijuana—legally used in their state. The federal DOT says: still fired. Which law wins?

buildup.text: Employers may test for drugs pre-employment, randomly, or after incidents—depending on state law and industry regulations.

discovery.text: Safety-sensitive industries—transportation, healthcare, construction—have stricter federal testing requirements.

twist.text: Marijuana legalization created chaos—state law allows it, federal law bans it, and employers are caught in the middle.

climax.text: Many companies now test for impairment rather than presence—focusing on safety, not off-duty behavior.

punchline.text: The question isn't what employees do at home. It's whether they're safe at work.

quiz.question: What conflict has marijuana legalization created for employers?
options: State law allows it but federal law still bans it
All employers must now allow marijuana
